_comment: Employees Retirement System of Texas (ERS) — State of Texas Retirement. Groups 1–3 defined benefit; Group 4 cash balance. Consumer-agnostic data only.
administeredBy: ERS — governed by a 6-member Board of Trustees
legalAuthority: Texas Government Code, Title 8 (Chapters 811–815 and related); Article XVI, Section 67, Texas Constitution

totalMembers.note: As of FY2023 (year ended Aug. 31, 2023). ERS also administers the Texas Employees Group Benefits Program (GBP) for ~538,000 employees, retirees, and dependents.

populations: Employees of State of Texas agencies
Certified peace officers and custodial officers eligible for LECOS Fund
Elected state officials, including district and criminal district attorneys receiving state general revenue salaries
Eligible state judges and justices (Judicial Retirement System Plan 2)
exclusions: Employees of Texas higher education institutions — covered by TRS or Optional Retirement Program (ORP); ERS administers their insurance benefits only
Employees of Community Supervision and Corrections Department — not administered by ERS for annuity purposes
Employees of Windham School District — not administered by ERS for annuity purposes
coverage.note: Participation in State of Texas Retirement is mandatory for all covered state agency employees. The first contribution is deducted from the first paycheck.

socialSecurity.note: Most State of Texas agency employees participate in federal Social Security in addition to ERS retirement. ERS encourages a three-legged stool approach: ERS pension + Social Security + personal savings (Texa$aver 401k/457).

vesting.note: Groups 1–4 regular account: fully vested after 5 years of contributing service. LECO Supplemental account (Group 4): fully vested after 20 years in a LECO position. Vesting means the member can begin receiving pension benefits if retirement eligibility is met.

groups1to3.note: Members hired before Sept. 1, 2022 (Groups 1, 2, 3) contribute 9.5% of salary pre-tax. State and agency employers together contribute 10% of salary to the ERS Retirement Trust Fund.

group4.note: Members hired on or after Sept. 1, 2022 (Group 4) contribute 6% of salary pre-tax. State match of 150% of entire account balance (contributions + interest + gain-sharing) applied at retirement — not credited monthly.

lecos.note: For certified law enforcement and custodial officers (LECOs): state contributes an additional 1.75% of salary to the LECOS Retirement Fund (Groups 1–3). Group 4 LECOs contribute an additional 2% to a supplemental LECO account; state applies a 300% match of the LECO account balance at retirement.

retirementGroups.overview: ERS has four retirement groups based on the date the member began contributing to a State of Texas agency. If a member leaves, withdraws their account, and returns, their group is based on the return-to-work date. Buying back withdrawn service credit does not restore the prior group.

formula.structure: 2.3% × years and months of service × highest average salary
formula.note: Standard multiplier is 2.3% for general employees. For certified LECOs with 20+ years of LECO service, the multiplier is 2.8% applied to LECO service years.

ruleOf80.note: Age (in years and months) plus years and months of service credit must equal 80 or more. Minimum 10 years of service required. No early retirement reduction for Group 1 under Rule of 80.

retirementEligibility.note: Group 1 members can use unused sick and annual leave toward both retirement eligibility and annuity calculation when retiring directly from state employment.

formula.structure: 2.3% × years and months of service × highest average salary

ruleOf80.note: Age plus service must equal 80 or more with minimum 10 years service.

alternativeEligibility.note: Optional benefits and health insurance available at age 65 if retiring at age 60 without meeting Rule of 80.

earlyRetirementReduction.note: Group 2 members who retire before age 60 (e.g., via Rule of 80 under age 60) receive a permanent 5% annuity reduction for each year under age 60 at retirement. Reduction is capped at 25%.
earlyRetirementReduction.example: A Group 2 member meeting Rule of 80 at age 57 retires with a 15% permanent annuity reduction.

formula.structure: 2.3% × years and months of service × highest average salary

ruleOf80.note: Age plus service must equal 80 or more with minimum 10 years service.

earlyRetirementReduction.note: Group 3 members who retire before age 62 (e.g., via Rule of 80 under age 62) receive a permanent 5% annuity reduction for each year under age 62 at retirement. There is no cap on the reduction.
earlyRetirementReduction.example: A Group 3 member meeting Rule of 80 at age 59 retires with a 15% permanent annuity reduction. A member retiring at age 56 via Rule of 80 would have a 30% reduction.

group4.planTypeNote: Group 4 is a cash balance benefit — a defined benefit structure in which the lifetime annuity is based on the account balance at retirement. It is not a 401(k); the monthly annuity is guaranteed for life regardless of investment performance after retirement.

gainSharing.formula: Half of the excess of the 5-year average ERS Trust Fund return over 4%, capped at 3%
gainSharing.note: Gain-share is applied to Aug. 31 account balances in December each year. Account can grow as much as 7% per year but never less than 4%.

stateMatchAtRetirement.note: State adds 150% of the entire account balance (contributions + interest + gain-sharing) at retirement. This match is not credited incrementally; it is applied when the member retires.

ruleOf80.note: Age plus years and months of service credit must equal 80 or more, with a minimum 5 years of service. No early retirement reduction for Group 4 under Rule of 80.

group4.gainSharingRetireesNote: Group 4 retirees receive the same gain-sharing applied to their account at retirement as active members. Once a gain-share increase is applied to the annuity, the annuity is never reduced.

lecos_group4.note: Group 4 LECOs contribute an additional 2% to a separate LECO supplemental account. The state applies a 300% match of the entire LECO account balance at retirement. LECO account vests after 20 years in LECO positions.

groups1to3.note: No guaranteed COLA for Groups 1–3. Annuity increases require authorization by the Texas Legislature. ERS does not guarantee regular annuity increases for Groups 1–3 retirees.

group4.note: Group 4 retirees may receive gain-sharing annuity increases of up to 3% per year when ERS investment returns allow. These increases are permanent — once applied, the annuity is never reduced.

lecos.overview: The Law Enforcement and Custodial Officer Supplemental (LECOS) Retirement Fund provides an additional retirement benefit for certified peace officers and custodial officers at ERS-covered state agencies.
lecos.eligibility: Certified law enforcement officers and custodial officers as defined by Texas Government Code who work at ERS-covered state agencies.
lecos.groups1to3Formula: 2.8% multiplier (vs. 2.3% standard) for LECO service years, requires 20+ years of LECO service credit

fundedStatus_aug2024.note: LECOSRF funded ratio was 101.5% (surplus of $27.8 million) as of August 31, 2024, up from 100.0% in FY2023. Fully funded after $772 million appropriation from Texas Legislature (House Bill 1) eliminated prior unfunded liability.

retirementInsurance.eligibilityRequirement: At least 10 years of eligible service credit with state agencies/higher education institutions participating in the Texas Employees Group Benefits Program (GBP).
retirementInsurance.grandfatherException: Members with at least 5 years of GBP participation as of September 1, 2014 are not subject to tiered retiree health insurance premiums.

tieredStatePremiumContribution.note: State pays 50%, 75%, or 100% of health insurance premium for full-time retirees based on eligible service credit. Part-time retirees receive half these amounts.

retirementInsurance.note: All four retirement groups have the same requirements to be eligible for retiree insurance. Health and other insurance benefits are subject to change based on available state funding — the Legislature has no continuing obligation to provide these benefits beyond each fiscal year.

supplementalSavings.name: Texa$aver 401(k) / 457 Program

autoEnrollment.note: Eligible state agency employees are automatically enrolled at 1% contribution in a target date fund. Employees may opt out or adjust contributions at any time.

supplementalSavings.note: ERS encourages employees to supplement State of Texas Retirement and Social Security with personal savings through Texa$aver. Average ERS annuity is approximately half of working salary.

trustFund.marketValueNote: As of early 2026, ERS manages pension fund assets of more than $45 billion. The fund crossed $40 billion for the first time in September 2024.
trustFund.note: Per SB 321 (passed 2021), ERS pension plan is on track to reach fully funded status by Aug. 31, 2054. The Legislature has made additional appropriations (totaling $1.53B+ under SB 321 and additional funds in subsequent sessions) to accelerate liability reduction.

sources.title: Retirement Annuity and Insurance Eligibility for Regular State Agency Employees
LECOS Retirement Fund Actuarial Valuation Aug. 31, 2024
ERS Trust Fund Exceeds $40 Billion News Release (Sept. 2024)
